steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure
steps.content: The blank contains a common noun immediately followed by Spain, a proper noun. Spain must specify which member nation is being discussed.

steps.title: Apply the rule and find the answer
steps.content: A proper noun that identifies which specific entity is being discussed requires no punctuation when placed directly after its common noun; Spain is introduced here for the first time to specify which OECD member nation, making it essential information. A comma would wrongly treat Spain as extra information, and hyphens and colons don't apply to noun-identification structures. This matches choice A — A is correct.
